name: Ombria del Benicadell Loop
name_original: L'Ombría del Benicadell — Circular desde Atzeneta d'Albaida
description: Explore the L'Ombría del Benicadell Protected Landscape on this moderate 18.8-mile mountain bike loop from Atzeneta d'Albaida.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Atzeneta D'Albaida traverse a landscape characterized by limestone mountains and extensive pine woods. The region is situated at the foothills of the Sierra del Benicadell mountain range, offering varied terrain for cycling. Notable features include the Solana de Benicadell, known for its challenging ascents, and the historic Ruta del Agua, which follows an ancient irrigation canal. This diverse geography provides a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Atzeneta D'Albaida The most popular mountain bike trail is Ombria del Benicadell Loop, a 18.8 miles (30.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This route explores the slopes of the Benicadell mountain, offering varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Path to Agres – Alt del Forner de Verda loop from Albaida, a moderate 15.1 miles (24.4 km) path. This route features a loop through the local countryside, including the Alt del Forner de Verda. Local mountain bikers also love the Alt del Forner de Verda – Pou Clar loop from Albaida, a 28.1 miles (45.3 km) trail leading through the area around Pou Clar, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Mountain biking around Atzeneta D'Albaida is defined by limestone mountains, pine woods, and ancient irrigation canals. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, with some routes featuring challenging ascents. The routes in Atzeneta D'Albaida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 3000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Atzeneta D'Albaida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: The mountain bike trails around Atzeneta D'Albaida offer a range of difficulties, from moderate to difficult. The region's limestone mountains and the challenging ascents of the Solana de Benicadell contribute to routes that test riders. While there are 8 easy routes, the majority of the 128 available trails are moderate (70) or difficult (50).

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: Yes, Atzeneta D'Albaida offers some easier options suitable for families. While many routes feature challenging ascents, there are 8 trails categorized as easy. These often follow gentler paths, such as those along the historic Ruta del Agua, which traces an ancient irrigation canal amidst refreshing natural surroundings.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: You'll encounter diverse landscapes, including limestone mountains, extensive pine woods, and stunning vistas across the valley. The region is at the foothills of the Sierra del Benicadell mountain range, with notable features like the rugged Solana de Benicadell. You might also ride alongside ancient irrigation canals, such as the Acequia del Port.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Atzeneta D'Albaida are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e916153272/ombria-del-benicadell-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ombria del Benicadell Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 18.8-mile (30.3 km) option exploring the mountain slopes. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21945827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path to Agres – Alt del Forner de Verda loop from Albaida&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 15.1-mile (24.4 km) trail through the countryside.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region, known as 'El Mirador del Valle' (The Valley Viewpoint), offers many scenic spots. You can find stunning views from the Solana de Benicadell. Additionally, nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3485518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;, which can be part of routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31536242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt del Forner de Verda – Pou Clar loop from Albaida&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4925262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Beniarrés reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4836497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sempere Reservoir Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: The Region of Valencia, where Atzeneta D'Albaida is located, generally offers a good climate for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures for tackling the varied terrain. While summer can be warm, early mornings or late afternoons are often suitable. Winters are typically mild, allowing for year-round riding, though some higher elevations might be cooler.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders looking for longer challenges will find several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33935343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Area – Camino del Torrater loop from Albaida&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 28.3-mile (45.6 km) route with significant elevation gain. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31974581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geodetic Point – Viewpoint at the Stone Pillar loop from Albaida&lt;/a&gt;, covering 26.9 miles (43.4 km) with over 850 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: The trails in Atzeneta D'Albaida are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging ascents, and the rewarding panoramic views from elevated points like the Solana de Benicadell. The well-maintained network of trails and the natural beauty of the limestone mountains and pine woods are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Atzeneta D'Albaida offers historical and cultural sites. The village itself has traditional Moorish urban planning. You can find the 18th-century San Juan Bautista Church and the El Santísimo Cristo de la Fe y del Monte Calvario chapel. Historically significant sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2546615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cava Gran de Agres&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient snow storage facility, are also nearby.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Atzeneta D'Albaida area?
A: There are over 120 mountain bike trails recorded in the Atzeneta D'Albaida area on komoot. This extensive network provides a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences, from shorter, easier rides to challenging, long-distance routes with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink after my ride in Atzeneta D'Albaida?
A: Yes, Atzeneta D'Albaida and the surrounding villages offer local gastronomy. After your ride, you can explore local eateries to enjoy specialties like &lt;em&gt;cassola al forn&lt;/em&gt; or &lt;em&gt;arroz caldoso&lt;/em&gt;, providing a taste of the local culture and a chance to refuel.
tips: Road in very good condition with incredible views. There are several access points for hiking along the way. Cyclable for mtb or gravel bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the L'Ombría del Benicadell circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail is easily accessible from Atzeneta d'Albaida. You'll find parking available near the starting point in the village, making it convenient to begin your mountain biking adventure.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied terrain, primarily consisting of a well-maintained forest track suitable for mountain and gravel bikes. You'll also encounter some paved sections, broken paths, and fun singletrack descents, particularly after passing a small cave and leading to the "Font del Anoueret" and towards Carrícola.

faqs.question: Is the L'Ombría del Benicadell - Circular from Atzeneta d'Albaida trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While it requires good physical fitness due to its length and elevation gain, it's generally suitable for riders with some mountain biking experience. Beginners might find certain sections challenging, especially the singletrack descents.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy stunning views of the impressive Benicadell peak, the valleys of Albaida and Comtat, and even the Beniarrés reservoir. The northern face, known as L'Ombría, is particularly picturesque with its beautiful pine forests. A notable highlight is the L'Ombria Forest Track of Benicadell, which forms a significant part of the journey.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Ombria del Benicadell Protected Landscape?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Ombria del Benicadell Protected Landscape for this mountain bike route. It is open for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The northern slope of the Sierra de Benicadell, which the track traverses, is characterized by its cold and humid conditions. This contributes to the beautiful pine forests. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding, avoiding the heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a convenient spot to refill water along the way. The L'Ombria Forest Track of Benicadell passes by the Font de la Solano, which can be used for refilling your water bottles.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails, including the Camino del Alba, El Camí IT5TR5 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, and various segments of the Pista Forestal de l'Ombria de Benicadell. You'll also ride parts of the Camí de la Font del Melero, Pista d'Otos, Camí de la Serra, and Camí del Corral de Diego.

faqs.question: Is the L'Ombría del Benicadell - Circular from Atzeneta d'Albaida trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, dogs are generally permitted on trails within protected landscapes like L'Ombría del Benicadell, provided they are kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find excellent panoramic vistas. One notable viewpoint is located behind the Casa Forestal de les Planisses, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscapes and the Benicadell peak.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 30.3 km long with around 890 meters of elevation gain. On average, it takes about 3 hours and 17 minutes to complete, but this can vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.
